articles.title: Circle rejected Ripple’s $5 billion buyout — now valued at over $20 billion after NYSE debut
articles.description: Circle, the USDC stablecoin issuer, made a blockbuster debut on Wall Street this week, with shares soaring to as high as $123, briefly pushing its market capitalization close to $25 billion. At the close of its second trading day on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), the stock (CRCL) settled at $107, giving Circle a valuation of more than $21 billion nearly quadrupling not only its IPO pricing of $31 per share but also a reported $5 billion buyout offer previously made by Ripple.
